title: fix(gen): surface upstream error codes instead of generic 500s
author: fisventurous

gist.headline: No more mystery 500s
gist.blurb: We taught our video API to stop panicking and actually tell you what went wrong. You'll now get proper status codes instead of generic 500s.
gist.summary: Video model handlers now properly surface upstream HTTP error codes instead of masking all failures as generic 500 errors.
gist.impact: Developers can now programmatically distinguish between content policy rejections, rate limits, and provider outages.
gist.visual_concept: The Robot and the Bee organizing error codes. The Robot is looking at a screen, and the Bee is sorting distinct error blocks (400, 429) instead of a giant generic 500 block.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of a chunky robot with a CRT monitor head holding a magnifying glass over a glowing server log, while the cute bee mascot organizes colorful blocks labeled '400', '429', and '502'. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.

image.prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of a chunky robot with a CRT monitor head holding a magnifying glass over a glowing server log, while the cute bee mascot organizes colorful blocks labeled '400', '429', and '502'.
